article_title: Ancient DNA From a Beijing Burial Site Reveals a Previously Unknown Human Lineage That Vanished From Modern East Asian Genomes

content_preview.title: Ancient DNA From a Beijing Burial Site Reveals a Previously Unknown Human Lineage That Vanished From Modern East Asian Genomes
content_preview.summary: Genome-wide analysis of 11,000-year-old remains from the Donghulin site near Beijing has identified a deeply divergent northern East Asian lineage with no match in any previously studied population, offering new evidence that cultural continuity does not guarantee genetic continuity.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A team of researchers led by Qiaomei Fu at the Institute of Vertebrate Paleontology and Paleoanthropology (IVPP) in Beijing has sequenced ancient DNA from three individuals buried at the Donghulin archaeological site in western Beijing, uncovering a human lineage that does not match any previously identified population. The findings, [published in Current Biology on March 23, 2026](https://www.cell.com/current-biology/fulltext/S0960-9822(26)00153-3), provide the earliest genetic evi...
